git.description: Git version control — read commit history, diffs, branches, create commits
git.when_to_use: When you need to understand code changes, review history, or verify commits

mongodb.description: MongoDB database — query exhibits, combinations, templates, pricing data
mongodb.when_to_use: When you need to verify data, check combinations, audit pricing changes, or test migrations

notes: These integrations allow Claude to read git history and query MongoDB directly for testing and verification. Both are read-focused by default (no write access recommended for safety).
